title: Document the Oban-Sandbox commit visibility trap
prompt: Write `docs/oban_sandbox_commit_trap.md` explaining why an Oban test that inserts a real job via `Oban.insert/1` and then calls `Oban.drain_queue/1` NEVER finds the job. Must explain: (1) the test process owns a sandbox transaction that is rolled back at the end, (2) `Oban.insert/1` inserts the job INSIDE the test's transaction (not committed), (3) `Oban.drain_queue/1` runs in a DIFFERENT process (the Oban queue worker), (4) that worker reads from the DB OUTSIDE the test's transaction, so it sees zero committed jobs, (5) fix: use `testing: :manual` + `perform_job/3` instead.
tier_rationale: Deep explanation of commit-visibility semantics — two processes with separate views.
research_citation: "Sandbox transactions never commit. If they never commit, then the rows are never visible to other processes, and that means they are never visible to Oban." — Elixir Forum, benwilson512
